steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content: In any triangle, the sum of the interior angles is 180°. For triangle EFG: $$\angle E + \angle F + \angle G = 180°$$ Given that $\angle E + \angle F = 31°$, we substitute: $$31° + \angle G = 180°$$ $$\angle G = 180° - 31° = 149°$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
